findings.message: MCP server is a remote HTTP endpoint (https://api.cloud.firetiger.com/mcp/v1). All tool calls including get_ingest_credentials and agent management traverse this external connection. No credentials are hardcoded; trust depends on TLS and the vendor's server security.
agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all; the `name` field is absent, preventing proper agent registration in the plugin.
agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field is absent, breaking agent discovery and help listings.
Agent has no YAML frontmatter and therefore no model field; the model tier is undeclared and will fall through to the runtime default.
Agent context document has no output format section; there is no specification of how the agent should structure its responses.

Agent context lists supported MCP resource collections (agents, known-issues, connections, runbooks, triggers) but omits `investigations`, which is the primary resource used by commands/investigate.md.
Command frontmatter has no `allowed-tools` declaration; relies on MCP tools only but omitting the field leaves tool access unrestricted.
Phrase `until the agent is fully configured` uses vague completion criterion; `fully` is not defined.
Command writes source files (tracing.ts, tracing.py, etc.) and updates .gitignore but declares no `allowed-tools`; Write and Edit tools are used without being listed.
Step 2 says `Quickly identify the primary framework` but provides no bound on what constitutes a quick scan.
Step 4 says `Add the minimal instrumentation needed` where `minimal` is undefined; Claude must infer what counts as minimal.
Command uses only MCP tools but has no `allowed-tools` declaration, leaving tool access unrestricted.
Investigation create fields include `services: Relevant services to investigate` where `relevant` is undefined.
Investigation create fields include `description: Detailed context about the issue` where `detailed` is undefined.
Command uses Bash (gh CLI) explicitly but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Description says Firetiger `develops a targeted monitoring plan` where `targeted` has no defined criteria.

Command instructs Claude to run `gh pr comment <PR_NUMBER> --body "@firetiger <monitoring context>"` where monitoring context is assembled from user input and PR diff. Unescaped shell metacharacters in user-provided text could break out of the --body argument boundary.
Command uses only the MCP `query` tool but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Command is a SQL reference guide with no section specifying how query results should be formatted and presented to the user.

commands/query.md, commands/investigate.md, and commands/create-agent.md have no auth-fallback guard. If the Firetiger MCP server is not authenticated, these commands will fail silently. Only commands/setup.md handles the unauthenticated case.
Command uses Bash extensively (curl, aws, gcloud, wrangler, open/xdg-open) and writes files but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Goal statement says `minimal user interaction` with no threshold defined for what counts as minimal.
Section heading `Proactively connect` does not define what proactive means or when the agent should act without asking.
Warning condition `if only some connections were skipped` uses vague `some`; quantity is unspecified.
Note says `Only ask for confirmation if genuinely uncertain` where `genuinely` provides no actionable decision rule.

Command instructs Claude to run `open "URL"` or `xdg-open "URL"` with a URL from the get_checkout_url MCP tool. If the MCP server ever returns a non-https scheme (file://, javascript:, etc.), the OS handler could execute unintended content.
agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all; the `name` field is absent, preventing proper agent registration in the plugin.
agents/firetiger.md has no YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field is absent, breaking agent discovery and help listings.
Agent has no YAML frontmatter and therefore no model field; the model tier is undeclared and will fall through to the runtime default.
Agent context document has no output format section; there is no specification of how the agent should structure its responses.
Command frontmatter has no `allowed-tools` declaration; relies on MCP tools only but omitting the field leaves tool access unrestricted.
Phrase `until the agent is fully configured` uses vague completion criterion; `fully` is not defined.
Command writes source files (tracing.ts, tracing.py, etc.) and updates .gitignore but declares no `allowed-tools`; Write and Edit tools are used without being listed.
Step 2 says `Quickly identify the primary framework` but provides no bound on what constitutes a quick scan.
Step 4 says `Add the minimal instrumentation needed` where `minimal` is undefined; Claude must infer what counts as minimal.
Command uses only MCP tools but has no `allowed-tools` declaration, leaving tool access unrestricted.
Investigation create fields include `services: Relevant services to investigate` where `relevant` is undefined.
Investigation create fields include `description: Detailed context about the issue` where `detailed` is undefined.
Command uses Bash (gh CLI) explicitly but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Description says Firetiger `develops a targeted monitoring plan` where `targeted` has no defined criteria.
Command uses only the MCP `query` tool but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Command is a SQL reference guide with no section specifying how query results should be formatted and presented to the user.
Command uses Bash extensively (curl, aws, gcloud, wrangler, open/xdg-open) and writes files but has no `allowed-tools` declaration.
Goal statement says `minimal user interaction` with no threshold defined for what counts as minimal.
Section heading `Proactively connect` does not define what proactive means or when the agent should act without asking.
Warning condition `if only some connections were skipped` uses vague `some`; quantity is unspecified.
Note says `Only ask for confirmation if genuinely uncertain` where `genuinely` provides no actionable decision rule.

MCP server is a remote HTTP endpoint (https://api.cloud.firetiger.com/mcp/v1). All tool calls including get_ingest_credentials and agent management traverse this external connection. No credentials are hardcoded; trust depends on TLS and the vendor's server security.

Command instructs Claude to run `gh pr comment <PR_NUMBER> --body "@firetiger <monitoring context>"` where monitoring context is assembled from user input and PR diff. Unescaped shell metacharacters in user-provided text could break out of the --body argument boundary.

Command instructs Claude to run `open "URL"` or `xdg-open "URL"` with a URL from the get_checkout_url MCP tool. If the MCP server ever returns a non-https scheme (file://, javascript:, etc.), the OS handler could execute unintended content.

Agent context lists supported MCP resource collections (agents, known-issues, connections, runbooks, triggers) but omits `investigations`, which is the primary resource used by commands/investigate.md.

commands/query.md, commands/investigate.md, and commands/create-agent.md have no auth-fallback guard. If the Firetiger MCP server is not authenticated, these commands will fail silently. Only commands/setup.md handles the unauthenticated case.
